skill.name: tailwind-patterns
skill.description: A Tailwind CSS v4 reference for CSS-first configuration, responsive layouts, design tokens, dark mode, and component patterns.
skill.summary: Apply modern Tailwind CSS v4 patterns with CSS-first tokens and responsive component guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, responsive prefixes, color names, and system font examples as command, cryptography, and reconnaissance risks. Manual review found no executable code, network access, secret handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent in SKILL.md. The only retained concern is low-risk filesystem access because the skill declares read and write editing tools.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Editing Tools Declared
low_findings.description: The skill declares Read, Write, Edit, Glob, and Grep in its allowed tools. This supports legitimate Tailwind implementation work, but users should understand that the skill may read and modify project files when invoked.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools are explicitly listed in the skill frontmatter. The risk is low because no malicious instructions or unauthorized data handling were found.

content.value_statement: Tailwind CSS v4 changes how teams configure themes, layouts, and responsive components. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for CSS-first Tailwind work.
actual_capabilities: Explains Tailwind CSS v4 CSS-first configuration with theme tokens.
Guides responsive design choices with breakpoints and container queries.
Provides layout patterns for flexbox, grid, and sidebar interfaces.
Recommends color token architecture with OKLCH and semantic naming.
Covers dark mode strategies, typography scales, animation utilities, and component extraction.
Identifies Tailwind anti-patterns such as excessive arbitrary values and heavy @apply usage.
limitations: It is a reference guide, not a Tailwind compiler or validator.
It does not include project-specific design requirements or brand tokens.
It assumes Tailwind CSS v4 concepts and may not fit older v3 projects.
It does not test generated UI changes in a browser by itself.

use_cases.title: Modernize Tailwind Configuration
use_cases.description: Move a frontend project toward CSS-first theme tokens and reduce reliance on legacy configuration files.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design System Utilities
use_cases.description: Create consistent token names, typography choices, color layers, and component extraction rules.

use_cases.title: Review Responsive UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Check whether a component should use viewport breakpoints, container queries, grid, or flexbox.

prompt_templates.title: Review A Tailwind Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component against Tailwind CSS v4 patterns. Focus on responsive behavior, token usage, dark mode, and avoid unnecessary refactors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an existing component

prompt_templates.title: Plan A CSS-First Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Tailwind CSS v4 theme plan for this project. Include semantic tokens, spacing, typography, and color layers without writing final code yet.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Responsive Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this layout using Tailwind CSS v4 principles. Decide where container queries, breakpoints, flexbox, or grid should be used.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Tailwind Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Tailwind implementation for v4 migration risks, repeated class lists, heavy @apply usage, token gaps, and performance concerns.

output_examples.input: Review a card component that uses fixed widths and repeated visual classes.
output: Use container-aware responsive behavior for the card internals.
Move repeated visual choices into a reusable component or token layer.
Replace one-off spacing and color choices with named design system values.

output_examples.input: Plan dark mode for a dashboard using Tailwind CSS v4.
output: Choose a manual theme strategy if users need a switcher.
Define semantic surface, text, and border tokens for light and dark themes.
Keep component classes focused on purpose instead of raw color names.

output_examples.input: Audit a Tailwind v3 project before migration.
output: Identify configuration that should move into CSS-first theme definitions.
Find repeated utility groups that should become components.
Flag heavy directive usage and arbitrary values that weaken consistency.

best_practices: Start with mobile styles, then add larger responsive behavior only where needed.
Use semantic design tokens for product meaning instead of raw visual values.
Prefer component extraction when repeated class combinations appear across the interface.
anti_patterns: Do not mix Tailwind v3 configuration habits with v4 CSS-first architecture.
Do not use arbitrary values as the default design system strategy.
Do not rely on heavy @apply usage when a framework component would be clearer.

faq.question: Does this skill generate Tailwind code?
faq.answer: It can guide Tailwind code decisions, but it is mainly a pattern reference for CSS-first Tailwind CSS v4 work.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to Tailwind CSS v4?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on v4 concepts such as CSS-first configuration, native container queries, and modern token patterns.

faq.question: Can it help migrate a v3 project?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify legacy patterns and suggest v4-oriented changes, but it does not automate a full migration.

faq.question: Does it include security-sensitive behavior?
faq.answer: No malicious behavior was found. The skill declares file editing tools, so users should review proposed edits before applying them.

faq.question: Can designers use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains token architecture, responsive patterns, typography, color systems, and component extraction in practical terms.

faq.question: Does it validate rendered UI?
faq.answer: No. It does not run browser tests or visual checks unless the user combines it with separate project tooling.
